:HL["/_next/static/chunks/b342db4cc7554694.css","style"]
:HL["/_next/static/media/797e433ab948586e-s.p.dbea232f.woff2","font",{"crossOrigin":"","type":"font/woff2"}]
:HL["/_next/static/media/caa3a2e1cccd8315-s.p.853070df.woff2","font",{"crossOrigin":"","type":"font/woff2"}]

4:["$","div",null,{"className":"min-h-screen bg-white dark:bg-zinc-950","children":[["$","header",null,{"className":"sticky top-0 z-50 bg-white/80 dark:bg-zinc-900/80 backdrop-blur-md border-b transition-colors","children":"Templates"}],["$","$Ld","/guides",{"href":"/guides","className":"text-sm font-medium text-zinc-600 dark:text-zinc-400 hover:text-zinc-900 dark:hover:text-white transition-colors","children":"Guides"}],["$","$Ld","/search",{"href":"/search","className":"text-sm font-medium text-zinc-600 dark:text-zinc-400 hover:text-zinc-900 dark:hover:text-white transition-colors","children":"Search"}]]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"href":"/search","className":"md:hidden p-2 text-zinc-600 24","children":["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","strokeWidth":2,"d":"M21 21l-6-6m2-5a7 7 0 11-14 0 7 7 0 0114 0z"}]}]}]]}]}]}],["$","main",null,{"children":[["$","script",null,{"type":"application/ld+json","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"{\"@context\":\"https://schema.org\",\"@type\":\"Article\",\"headline\":\"Best OpenClaw Tools for Beginners\",\"description\":\"Discover the essential OpenClaw tools every beginner should know. From web search to file management, these tools will supercharge your AI agents.\",\"image\":\"/images/openclaw-tools.png\",\"datePublished\":\"2026-03-14\",\"dateModified\":\"2026-03-14\",\"author\":{\"@type\":\"Organization\",\"name\":\"OpenClaw Team\"},\"publisher\":{\"@type\":\"Organization\",\"name\":\"OpenClaw Wiki\",\"logo\":{\"@type\":\"ImageObject\",\"url\":\"https://openclawwiki.com/logo.png\"}},\"keywords\":\"OpenClaw tools, AI agent tools, beginner tools, automation tools\"}"}}],["$","script",null,{"type":"application/ld+json","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"{\"@context\":\"https://schema.org\",\"@type\":\"BreadcrumbList\",\"itemListElement\":[{\"@type\":\"ListItem\",\"position\":1,\"name\":\"Home\",\"item\":\"https://openclawwiki.com\"},{\"@type\":\"ListItem\",\"position\":2,\"name\":\"tools\",\"item\":\"https://openclawwiki.com/tools\"},{\"@type\":\"ListItem\",\"position\":3,\"name\":\"Best OpenClaw Tools for Beginners\",\"item\":\"https://openclawwiki.com/posts/best-openclaw-tools-beginners\"}]}"}}],["$","div",null,{"className":"max-w-4xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8 pt-8","children":["$","nav",null,{"className":"flex items-center text-sm text-zinc-500 dark:text-zinc-400","children":[["$","$Ld",null,{"href":"/","className":"hover:text-zinc-900 dark:hover:text-white","children":"Home"}],["$","span",null,{"className":"mx-2","children":"/"}],"$Le","$Lf","$L10"]}]}],"$L11","$L12"]}],"$L13"]}]
:HL["/images/openclaw-tools.png","image"]
:HL["/images/first-agent.png","image"]
:HL["/images/install-openclaw.png","image"]
:HL["/images/openclaw-intro.png","image"]
e:["$","$Ld",null,{"href":"/tools","className":"capitalize hover:text-zinc-900 dark:hover:text-white","children":"tools"}]
f:["$","span",null,{"className":"mx-2","children":"/"}]
10:["$","span",null,{"className":"text-zinc-900 dark:text-white truncate","children":"Best OpenClaw Tools for Beginners"}]
14:T2c33,<h2>Introduction</h2>
<p>One of OpenClaw's greatest strengths is its extensive library of tools that extend your agents' capabilities. This guide covers the essential tools every beginner should know.</p>
<h2>What Are OpenClaw Tools?</h2>
<p>Tools in OpenClaw are pre-built integrations that allow your agents to interact with external services and perform specific tasks. Think of them as "superpowers" you can give your agents.</p>
<h2>Essential Tools for Beginners</h2>
<h3>1. Web Search</h3>
<p><strong>What it does</strong>: Allows your agent to search the internet for current information.</p>
<p><strong>Use cases</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>Research topics</li>
<li>Find current news</li>
<li>Verify facts</li>
<li>Gather data</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>How to use</strong>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml"># In your agent configuration
capabilities:
  - web_search
</code></pre>
<p><strong>Example</strong>:</p>
<pre><code>User: "What's the weather in Tokyo?"
Agent: [Uses web_search to find current weather]
</code></pre>
<p><strong>Pro tip</strong>: Combine with memory so your agent remembers search results for follow-up questions.</p>
<h3>2. Calculator</h3>
<p><strong>What it does</strong>: Performs mathematical calculations.</p>
<p><strong>Use cases</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>Complex calculations</li>
<li>Unit conversions</li>
<li>Financial math</li>
<li>Data analysis</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>How to use</strong>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">capabilities:
  - calculator
</code></pre>
<p><strong>Example</strong>:</p>
<pre><code>User: "What's 15% of $450?"
Agent: "15% of $450 is $67.50"
</code></pre>
<h3>3. File Operations</h3>
<p><strong>What it does</strong>: Read, write, and manipulate files.</p>
<p><strong>Use cases</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>Save conversation logs</li>
<li>Read configuration files</li>
<li>Process data files</li>
<li>Generate reports</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>How to use</strong>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">capabilities:
  - file_read
  - file_write
</code></pre>
<p><strong>Example</strong>:</p>
<pre><code>User: "Save this summary to notes.txt"
Agent: [Writes summary to file]
</code></pre>
<h3>4. Date and Time</h3>
<p><strong>What it does</strong>: Get current time, calculate dates, handle time zones.</p>
<p><strong>Use cases</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>Scheduling</li>
<li>Reminders</li>
<li>Time zone conversions</li>
<li>Date calculations</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>How to use</strong>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">capabilities:
  - datetime
</code></pre>
<p><strong>Example</strong>:</p>
<pre><code>User: "What time is it in London?"
Agent: "It's currently 3:45 PM in London"
</code></pre>
<h3>5. Email</h3>
<p><strong>What it does</strong>: Send and read emails.</p>
<p><strong>Use cases</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>Notifications</li>
<li>Report delivery</li>
<li>Communication automation</li>
<li>Alert systems</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>How to use</strong>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">capabilities:
  - email_send
</code></pre>
<p><strong>Setup required</strong>: Configure email credentials in settings.</p>
<p><strong>Example</strong>:</p>
<pre><code>User: "Email the report to the team"
Agent: [Sends email with report attached]
</code></pre>
<h3>6. Database Query</h3>
<p><strong>What it does</strong>: Connect to and query databases.</p>
<p><strong>Use cases</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>Data retrieval</li>
<li>Report generation</li>
<li>Analytics</li>
<li>User management</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>How to use</strong>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">capabilities:
  - database_query
</code></pre>
<p><strong>Supported databases</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>PostgreSQL</li>
<li>MySQL</li>
<li>SQLite</li>
<li>MongoDB</li>
</ul>
<h3>7. API Client</h3>
<p><strong>What it does</strong>: Make HTTP requests to external APIs.</p>
<p><strong>Use cases</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>Integrate with third-party services</li>
<li>Fetch data from REST APIs</li>
<li>Webhook handling</li>
<li>Microservice communication</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>How to use</strong>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">capabilities:
  - http_request
</code></pre>
<p><strong>Example</strong>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">tools:
  weather_api:
    type: http_request
    endpoint: https://api.weather.com/v1/current
    method: GET
</code></pre>
<h3>8. Text Processing</h3>
<p><strong>What it does</strong>: Analyze and transform text.</p>
<p><strong>Use cases</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>Summarization</li>
<li>Translation</li>
<li>Sentiment analysis</li>
<li>Text extraction</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>How to use</strong>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">capabilities:
  - text_summarize
  - text_translate
</code></pre>
<h3>9. CSV/Data Processing</h3>
<p><strong>What it does</strong>: Read, write, and manipulate CSV files.</p>
<p><strong>Use cases</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>Data import/export</li>
<li>Report generation</li>
<li>Data transformation</li>
<li>Bulk operations</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>How to use</strong>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">capabilities:
  - csv_read
  - csv_write
</code></pre>
<h3>10. Memory/Storage</h3>
<p><strong>What it does</strong>: Persistent storage for agent data.</p>
<p><strong>Use cases</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>Remember user preferences</li>
<li>Store conversation history</li>
<li>Cache frequently accessed data</li>
<li>Maintain state between sessions</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>How to use</strong>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">memory:
  enabled: true
  type: persistent
</code></pre>
<h2>How to Enable Tools</h2>
<h3>Method 1: Agent Configuration</h3>
<p>Add to your agent's YAML file:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml"># agents/my-agent.yaml
name: My Assistant
capabilities:
  - web_search
  - calculator
  - file_read
  - file_write
</code></pre>
<h3>Method 2: Project Configuration</h3>
<p>Enable globally in <code>config.yaml</code>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">tools:
  enabled:
    - web_search
    - calculator
    - datetime
</code></pre>
<h3>Method 3: Runtime</h3>
<p>Enable dynamically in code:</p>
<pre><code class="language-javascript">const agent = new Agent({
  tools: ['web_search', 'calculator']
});
</code></pre>
<h2>Tool Configuration</h2>
<h3>Setting Up API Keys</h3>
<p>Some tools require API keys:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">openclaw config set tools.web_search.api_key YOUR_KEY
openclaw config set tools.email.smtp_password YOUR_PASSWORD
</code></pre>
<h3>Custom Tool Settings</h3>
<p>Configure tool behavior:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">tools:
  web_search:
    max_results: 5
    timeout: 10000
    safe_search: true
  
  calculator:
    precision: 10
    scientific: true
</code></pre>
<h2>Best Practices</h2>
<h3>1. Start Small</h3>
<p>Don't enable all tools at once. Start with 2-3 essential tools and add more as needed.</p>
<p><strong>Good</strong>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">capabilities:
  - web_search
  - calculator
</code></pre>
<p><strong>Too much</strong>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">capabilities:
  - web_search
  - calculator
  - file_read
  - file_write
  - email_send
  - database_query
  - http_request
  # ... 20 more tools
</code></pre>
<h3>2. Match Tools to Use Case</h3>
<p>Only enable tools relevant to your agent's purpose:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Research Agent</strong>: web_search, text_summarize</li>
<li><strong>Calculator Agent</strong>: calculator, unit_converter</li>
<li><strong>File Manager</strong>: file_read, file_write, csv_process</li>
</ul>
<h3>3. Configure Rate Limits</h3>
<p>Prevent abuse and control costs:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">tools:
  web_search:
    rate_limit:
      requests_per_minute: 10
</code></pre>
<h3>4. Handle Errors Gracefully</h3>
<p>Always expect tools to fail:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">agent:
  error_handling:
    on_tool_failure: "inform_user"
    fallback_message: "I'm sorry, I couldn't complete that action."
</code></pre>
<h2>Creating Custom Tools</h2>
<p>If built-in tools don't meet your needs, create custom ones:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml"># tools/my_custom_tool.yaml
name: my_tool
description: Does something specific

parameters:
  input:
    type: string
    required: true

handler: |
  // Custom logic here
  const result = process(params.input);
  return { success: true, data: result };
</code></pre>
<h2>Tool Combinations</h2>
<p>Powerful workflows come from combining tools:</p>
<h3>Example 1: Research Assistant</h3>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">capabilities:
  - web_search
  - text_summarize
  - file_write

workflow:
  1. Search for information
  2. Summarize findings
  3. Save to file
</code></pre>
<h3>Example 2: Data Pipeline</h3>
<pre><code class="language-yaml">capabilities:
  - http_request
  - csv_write
  - email_send

workflow:
  1. Fetch data from API
  2. Transform to CSV
  3. Email report
</code></pre>
<h2>Troubleshooting</h2>
<h3>Tool Not Working</h3>
<ol>
<li>Check if tool is enabled</li>
<li>Verify API keys are set</li>
<li>Check tool-specific configuration</li>
<li>Review error logs</li>
</ol>
<h3>Rate Limit Errors</h3>
<ul>
<li>Implement rate limiting</li>
<li>Add retry logic</li>
<li>Use caching</li>
<li>Upgrade plan if needed</li>
</ul>
<h3>Permission Errors</h3>
<ul>
<li>Check file permissions</li>
<li>Verify API credentials</li>
<li>Review OAuth scopes</li>
<li>Check firewall settings</li>
</ul>
<h2>Advanced Tools (Coming Soon)</h2>
<p>OpenClaw is constantly adding new tools:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Vision</strong>: Image analysis and generation</li>
<li><strong>Audio</strong>: Speech recognition and synthesis</li>
<li><strong>Code Execution</strong>: Run code safely</li>
<li><strong>Browser Automation</strong>: Control web browsers</li>
<li><strong>ML Models</strong>: Access specialized AI models</li>
</ul>
<h2>FAQ</h2>
<p><strong>Q: Are tools free to use?</strong></p>
<p>A: Built-in tools are free. Some third-party integrations may require API keys with their own pricing.</p>
<p><strong>Q: Can I create my own tools?</strong></p>
<p>A: Yes! OpenClaw supports custom tools via YAML or JavaScript/TypeScript.</p>
<p><strong>Q: How do I know which tools to enable?</strong></p>
<p>A: Start with your agent's core purpose and add tools that support those tasks.</p>
<p><strong>Q: Can tools be shared between agents?</strong></p>
<p>A: Yes, tools are configured at the project level and can be used by any agent.</p>
<p><strong>Q: Are there security concerns with tools?</strong></p>
<p>A: Yes, especially file and network tools. Always validate inputs and use sandboxing when possible.</p>
<h2>Conclusion</h2>
<p>Tools are what transform a simple chatbot into a powerful AI assistant. Start with the essentials, gradually add more capabilities, and don't be afraid to create custom tools for your specific needs.</p>
<p>Remember:</p>
<ul>
<li>Start small</li>
<li>Match tools to use case</li>
<li>Configure properly</li>
<li>Handle errors gracefully</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Ready to build?</strong> Check out our <a href="https://docs.openclaw.com/tools">complete tool reference</a> or <a href="/posts/build-first-ai-agent">build your first agent</a>.</p>
<h2>Related Articles</h2>
<ul>
<li><a href="/posts/build-first-ai-agent">Build Your First AI Agent</a></li>
<li><a href="/posts/openclaw-vs-crewai">OpenClaw vs CrewAI</a></li>
<li><a href="/posts/what-is-openclaw">What Is OpenClaw?</a></li>
</ul>
